description: A vertical graphic (same template/photo as another USCIS post) built on a real enforcement photograph with a slogan overlay. The photo shows an outdoor arrest scene: in the foreground a gray-haired, bearded detained man in a dark shirt stands with his back to the camera, hands cuffed behind his back; at right a law-enforcement officer seen from behind wears a green tactical/molle vest with a tan patch reading 'POLICE / ICE' and tan cargo pants with a holstered sidearm; a chain-link fence and green trees form the background. Overlaid on the lower-left is the standard solid-blue box with white serif text 'Making America Safe Again'. The composition uses an authentic handcuffing image to dramatize an arrest (the tweet describes a Nigerian national with an outstanding warrant for child cruelty and aggravated assault), lending documentary weight to the campaign slogan. The propaganda technique pairs a real arrest scene with the tagline to project successful USCIS/ICE enforcement 'one arrest at a time', targeting the general public. Designed agency graphic (photo with branded overlay).
notable_text: POLICE / ICE (officer's vest). Making America Safe Again.
